content: We can reframe zero-shot node generalization on graphs as a retrieval-augmented generation task, where contrastive pre-training learns to structure graph embeddings such that unlabeled target nodes 'retrieve' semantically compatible structural patterns from a pre-trained corpus of source graph node embeddings, rather than relying purely on linear projection to unseen classes. This allows zero-shot node models to leverage fine-grained structural commonalities between source and target domains instead of only learning coarse-grained embedding alignment.
source_topics: Contrastive pre-training strategies for zero-shot node generalization
trigger_topic: Contrastive pre-training strategies for zero-shot node generalization
